parsed_review.summary: CP00-686 is a descriptor-only RP22 External Integrations I P07 bridge pack (blocked-claim receipt, failure fixture/test, audit/Hermes evidence, Claude edge-case prompt, human escalation, synthetic failure taxonomy) over 10 planned units RP22.P07.M05.S15-RP22.P07.M06.S02 (risk A). The pack makes no production_ready or runtime claim (manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status in_progress/pending_review); its only assertion is a descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, runtime-closed bridge, and that assertion is fully supported. Registry binding/requirements, service descriptor+Hermes+Claude+handoff functions, validators (coverage + descriptor), model.test.js (2 blocks), the contract validation script, and both generated contract files (generated_from_pack_id=CP00-686) are mutually consistent with the manifest, baseline, prompt, and live plan (live_latest_pack_id=CP00-686, next CP00-687/RP22.P07.M06.S03). Distributions match exactly: deliverables {hermes_evidence:2,fixture:1,test:2,security_audit:1,claude_review:1,implementation:1,failure_recovery:2}=10 and micro-titles {Permission And Audit Binding:8,Synthetic Fixture Set:2}=10. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in freezeCp686Result (no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, all leak/runtime flags false) and asserted by validators (raw_payload_included:false per row). Claude stays read-only and non-final (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, read_only:true); Hermes is used only as a descriptor review/evidence packet (hermes_runtime_opened:false, runtime_receipt_emitted:false), not product runtime. Review-hardening preconditions hold: auth preflight passed with valid JSON, prompt 2496B under the 25600B limit, no full diff/source embedded, read-only tools only, no forbidden tools; command-evidence records all 22 commands (including the 10 required validators, git diff --check, and npm test) at exit_code 0. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. Per the authority-boundary guidance, Claude not being final approver does not block this CP closeout goal, and the pack makes no runtime/enterprise-trust claim, so blocks_goal_closeout is false. Verification was performed via read-only inspection of source and evidence; validation commands were not re-executed.
